content: This appears to be an occasional bug in Thunderbird's "experimental" Account Hub feature. Try turning it off and try setting up the account again. The setting is found at <Settings | General | Account Hub> . Uncheck "Create accounts in the new Account Hub".
creator: rednoise

content: How exactly do I find this Account Hub??? I do not see any place that allows me to turn off the "experimental" switch.
creator: RedRat

content:
I described above where to find the setting and how to disable it. Can you not see it?

To be more specific, it can be found at menu option (in Linux Mint) at <Edit | Settings | General | Account Hub>. If you have the menu bar hidden, you can reveal it by pressing Alt.

See the attached screenshot.

The fact that Thunderbird is running on other machines is irrelevant. You can have it going on any number of devices at any time. If everything is cranked up here at home, I could have as many as four desktop computers and a phone all checking Comcast for email at the same time.
